findings.description: Off-by-one in assemble(): range(total_chunks - 1) skips the final chunk (index total_chunks-1), so every multi-chunk upload is reassembled truncated/corrupt and a single-chunk upload yields a 0-byte file, yet the file is still stored and the session deleted.

assemble() never calls verify_checksum() or file_size_ok(); it stores the file and deletes/cleans up the session unconditionally, so the line-65 corruption (and any oversize/mismatch) is never caught by the handler itself and a bad file is persisted as if valid.

MAX_FILE_MB is effectively dead: chunks are written with no cumulative/per-chunk size check, and file_size_ok() is the only enforcement but is never invoked internally and only runs after the full file is already on disk, so the declared size limit is not actually enforced.

Unsanitized session.filename flows into os.path.join(UPLOAD_DIR, filename); a value like '../../etc/cron.d/evil.txt' passes the extension allowlist yet escapes UPLOAD_DIR (path traversal / arbitrary write), and an absolute path would replace UPLOAD_DIR entirely — noting in passing, defer detail to critic-security.
Non-atomic write with no existence check: two uploads sharing the same filename silently overwrite each other's stored file (no temp-file + rename, no collision guard), causing data loss.

No locking anywhere in the class (a web-service context implies concurrent requests); concurrent assemble()/abort() on the same upload_id race on self._sessions and the unguarded 'del self._sessions[upload_id]' can raise KeyError, and receive_chunk/start mutate shared state without synchronization.

Calling start() again with an already-active upload_id overwrites the existing UploadSession in self._sessions, orphaning that session's already-written chunk files on disk (resource leak) with no cleanup.

verify_checksum compares hexdigest() (always lowercase) to the client-supplied expected_sha256 case-sensitively, so a valid uppercase-hex digest from the client is wrongly rejected as a mismatch (false negative).

Unsanitized upload_id is interpolated into the chunk filename f'{upload_id}.{index}' and joined into CHUNK_DIR, allowing path traversal via the upload_id (e.g. containing '../' or path separators) — noting in passing, defer detail to critic-security.

verify_checksum reads the entire file into memory via f.read() (potentially 200MB+ given MAX_FILE_MB and the unenforced limit) instead of streaming in fixed-size blocks, risking memory exhaustion/DoS — noting in passing, defer detail to critic-performance.

No error handling for missing/unreadable files in verify_checksum, file_size_ok, or assemble's chunk opens; a missing stored_path or chunk file raises an uncaught OSError/FileNotFoundError instead of degrading gracefully or returning a failure result.
Checksum comparison uses '!=' (non-constant-time) rather than hmac.compare_digest, a minor timing-side-channel concern for integrity verification — noting in passing, defer to critic-security.
